seoTitle: Romaine vs Rose Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Romaine and Rose Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Romaine vs Rose Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Romaine and Rose Bay.
heading: Romaine vs Rose Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Romaine, Tasmania and Rose Bay, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Romaine has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Rose Bay (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Romaine or Rose Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Romaine has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Rose Bay scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Romaine scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Rose Bay scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Romaine or Rose Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Romaine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
